paper_map.title: Evaluation of the elastic modulus of steels
paper_map.abstract_summary: Literature data on the elastic modulus of steels are collected and analyzed as a function of chemical composition using regression analysis and thermodynamic calculations. Steels are divided into martensitic/ferritic, pure ferritic, and austenitic groups. Regression coefficients are determined for alloying elements and precipitates to predict the elastic modulus, revealing that cementite has no effect in ferritic steels while elements like Cr, Mo, Si, Mn increase it; in austenitic steels N, Cr, NbC, TiC increase and Ni, Mo decrease the modulus.
paper_map.research_objective: To quantify the influence of chemical composition and equilibrium phase constituents on the elastic modulus of steels via statistical regression and thermodynamic modeling, enabling composition-based prediction.
main_process_variables: chemical composition (mass content of Cr, Mo, Si, Mn, Cu, Ni, C, N, Nb, Ti)
phase composition (equilibrium phase fractions from Thermo-Calc)
temperature (room temperature, elevated temperatures up to 400°C)
